turns.prompt: Create a simple blog architecture with a NextJS frontend and a FastAPI backend.

services.name: Auth Service
services.responsibility: Handles author authentication (login, logout, session management) and authorization (token validation for content management actions).

services.name: Blog Post Service
services.responsibility: Manages all CRUD operations for blog posts (create, read, update, delete). Serves blog post content to readers and provides authoring APIs.

boundaries: Blog Post Service -> Auth Service (for internal authorization checks)
communication: HTTP/REST (for all client-service and inter-service communication)
persistence: PostgreSQL (Primary data store for author accounts, blog post content, and metadata, ensuring transactional integrity and data consistency)
Redis (In-memory data store for caching frequently accessed blog posts and potentially session tokens, improving read performance and reducing database load)
deployment: Containerization (Docker) for all backend services (Auth Service, Blog Post Service)
Kubernetes cluster (for orchestration, deployment, and management of containerized backend services)
Managed PostgreSQL service (e.g., AWS RDS, Azure Database for PostgreSQL, GCP Cloud SQL) for high availability and operational ease.
Managed Redis service (e.g., AWS ElastiCache, Azure Cache for Redis, GCP Memorystore) for caching.
API Gateway (e.g., Nginx, Envoy, or a cloud-managed API Gateway like AWS API Gateway) to route requests, enforce security, and provide a single entry point.
NextJS Frontend deployed on a platform supporting SSR/SSG (e.g., Vercel, or containerized on Kubernetes/serverless functions).
scalability: Horizontal auto-scaling for Auth Service and Blog Post Service instances based on CPU utilization or request load.
Database read replicas for PostgreSQL to distribute read load and improve read performance for readers.
Caching layer (Redis) to offload database reads for popular blog posts, significantly reducing latency.
Load balancing across multiple instances of each service to distribute incoming traffic evenly.
Potential future use of Content Delivery Network (CDN) for static assets or pre-rendered content to reduce latency for geographically dispersed readers.

turns.prompt: Change the backend to use Django instead of FastAPI.
